programme.title: The Terminator
programme.desc: In the post-apocalyptic future, reigning tyrannical supercomputers teleport a cyborg assassin known as the "Terminator" back to 1984 to kill Sarah Connor, whose unborn son is destined to lead insurgents against 21st century mechanical hegemony. Meanwhile, the human-resistance movement dispatches a lone warrior to safeguard Sarah. Can he stop the virtually indestructible killing machine?

programme.title: Snatch
programme.desc: Unscrupulous boxing promoters, violent bookmakers, a Russian gangster, incompetent amateur robbers and supposedly Jewish jewelers fight to track down a priceless stolen diamond.

programme.title: Abraham Lincoln: Vampire Hunter
programme.desc: Abraham Lincoln, the 16th President of the United States, discovers vampires are planning to take over the United States. He makes it his mission to eliminate them.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 4
programme.desc: John Wick uncovers a path to defeating The High Table. But before he can earn his freedom, Wick must face off against a new enemy with powerful alliances across the globe and forces that turn old friends into foes.

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
programme.desc: After stealing a mysterious orb in the far reaches of outer space, Peter Quill from Earth is now the main target of a manhunt led by the villain known as Ronan the Accuser. To help fight Ronan and his team and save the galaxy from his power, Quill creates a team of space heroes known as the "Guardians of the Galaxy" to save the galaxy.

programme.title: The Goonies
programme.desc: Young teen Mikey Walsh and his friends set off on a quest to find Pirate One-Eyed Willie's treasure in hopes of saving their homes from demolition. However, on their quest to find the treasure, they run into a family of recently escaped criminals, determined to capture the kids and reach the treasure first.

programme.title: Big Trouble in Little China
programme.desc: Truck driver Jack Burton gets embroiled in a supernatural battle when his best friend Wang Chi's green-eyed fiancée is kidnapped by henchmen of the sorcerer Lo Pan, who must marry a girl with green eyes in order to return to the human realm.

programme.title: Nobody
programme.desc: Hutch Mansell, a suburban dad, overlooked husband, nothing neighbor — a "nobody." When two thieves break into his home one night, Hutch's unknown long-simmering rage is ignited and propels him on a brutal path that will uncover dark secrets he fought to leave behind.

programme.title: Léon: The Professional
programme.desc: After her father, step-mother, step-sister and little brother are killed by her father's employers, the 12-year-old daughter of an abject drug dealer manages to take refuge in the apartment of a professional hitman who at her request teaches her the methods of his job so she can take her revenge on the corrupt DEA agent who ruined her life by killing her beloved brother.

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
programme.desc: After being snatched from the Green Place of Many Mothers, while the tyrants Dementus and Immortan Joe fight for power and control, the young Furiosa must survive many trials as she puts together the means to find her way home.

programme.title: Aliens
programme.desc: Ripley, the sole survivor of the Nostromo's deadly encounter with the monstrous Alien, returns to Earth after drifting through space in hypersleep for 57 years. Although her story is initially met with skepticism, she agrees to accompany a team of Colonial Marines back to LV-426.

programme.title: Ballerina
programme.desc: An assassin trained in the traditions of the Ruska Roma organization sets out to seek revenge after her father's death.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: My Blue Heaven
programme.desc: An all too uptight FBI agent must protect a larger than life mobster with a heart of gold, currently under witness protection in the suburbs.

programme.title: Police Academy 5: Assignment: Miami Beach
programme.desc: Eric Lassard is about to retire, which Harris plans to exploit to take over his position. In Miami, where Lassard is to be honored, a mix-up leads to an adventure involving diamonds, kidnapping and betrayal.

programme.title: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery
programme.desc: As a swinging fashion photographer by day and a groovy British superagent by night, Austin Powers is the '60s' most shagadelic spy. But can he stop megalomaniac Dr. Evil after the bald villain freezes himself and unthaws in the '90s? With the help of sexy sidekick Vanessa Kensington, he just might.

programme.title: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York
programme.desc: Kevin is separated from his family again when he accidentally boards a flight to New York City during a Christmas trip to Miami. However he crosses paths with the same burglars, who now plan to rob a toy store on Christmas Eve.

programme.title: Caddyshack
programme.desc: At an exclusive country club, an ambitious young caddy, Danny Noonan, eagerly pursues a caddy scholarship in hopes of attending college and, in turn, avoiding a job at the lumber yard. In order to succeed, he must first win the favour of the elitist Judge Smails, and then the caddy golf tournament which Smails sponsors.

programme.title: Napoleon Dynamite
programme.desc: Preston, Idaho's most curious resident, Napoleon Dynamite, lives with his grandma and his 32-year-old brother (who cruises chat rooms for ladies) and works to help his best friend, Pedro, snatch the Student Body President title from mean teen Summer Wheatley.

programme.title: Police Academy 6: City Under Siege
programme.desc: Our favourite police men are called together to deal with a gang who rob banks and jewelers. Using their various talents as well as their extraordinary luck, the crooks stand no chance against our men and women in blue.

programme.title: Revenge of the Nerds II: Nerds in Paradise
programme.desc: Get out your white socks! This time our suave college heroes hit the road for fun and fraternity in Florida. Another opportunity for brains to triumph over brawn. How can the girls possibly resist their geeky charms?

programme.title: Pee-wee's Big Adventure
programme.desc: The cartoonish and childish character Pee-wee Herman goes on a big adventure for the first time ever when his beloved shiny new bicycle is stolen by his nemesis Francis Buxton, a fellow man-child and neighborhood rich "kid." And he sets off on an obsessive cross-country journey, determined to recover it. Pee-wee's awkward and childish attempts to be cool and mature.

programme.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice
programme.desc: After a family tragedy, three generations of the Deetz family return home to Winter River. Still haunted by Beetlejuice, Lydia's life is turned upside down when her teenage daughter, Astrid, accidentally opens the portal to the Afterlife.

programme.title: Back to School
programme.desc: Millionaire businessman Thornton Melon is upset when his son Jason announces that he is not sure about going to college. Thornton insists that college is the best thing he never had for himself, and to prove his point, he agrees to enroll in school along with his son. Thornton is a big hit on campus: always throwing the biggest parties, knowing all the right people, but is this the way to pass college?

programme.title: Austin Powers in Goldmember
programme.desc: The world's most shagadelic spy continues his fight against Dr. Evil. This time, the diabolical doctor and his clone, Mini-Me, team up with a new foe—'70s kingpin Goldmember. While pursuing the team of villains to stop them from world domination, Austin gets help from his dad and an old girlfriend.

programme.title: Happy Gilmore
programme.desc: A Hockey player wannabe finds out that he has the most powerful golf drive in history. He joins the P.G.A. tour to make some money to save grandma's house. The downside is that his hockey player mentality doesn't really go on the P.G.A. tour. Especially with the favorite to win the championship.i

programme.title: The Toy
programme.desc: On one of his bratty son Eric's annual visits, the plutocrat U.S. Bates takes him to his department store and offers him anything in it as a gift. Eric chooses a black janitor who has made him laugh with his antics. At first the man suffers many indignities as Eric's "toy," but gradually teaches the lonely boy what it is like to have and to be a friend.

programme.title: Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me
programme.desc: When diabolical genius Dr. Evil travels back in time to steal superspy Austin Powers's ‘mojo,’ Austin must return to the swingin' '60s himself - with the help of American agent, Felicity Shagwell - to stop the dastardly plan. Once there, Austin faces off against Dr. Evil's army of minions to try to save the world in his own unbelievably groovy way.

programme.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail 1974
programme.desc: King Arthur, accompanied by his squire, recruits his Knights of the Round Table, including Sir Bedevere the Wise, Sir Lancelot the Brave, Sir Robin the Not-Quite-So-Brave-As-Sir-Lancelot and Sir Galahad the Pure. On the way, Arthur battles the Black Knight who, despite having had all his limbs chopped off, insists he can still fight. They reach Camelot, but Arthur decides not to enter, as "it is a silly place".

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Five grisly tales from a kid's comic book about a murdered father rising from his grave, a bizarre meteor, a vengeful husband, a mysterious crate's occupant, and a plague of cockroaches.

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Transylvanian vampire Count Dracula bends a naive real estate agent to his will, then takes up residence at a London estate where he sleeps in his coffin by day and searches for potential victims by night.

programme.title: Ed Wood
programme.desc: Ambitious but troubled movie director Edward D. Wood Jr. tries his best to fulfill his dreams despite his lack of talent.

programme.title: Frankenstein
programme.desc: The 1931 horror movie "Frankenstein", tells the tale of when Henry Frankenstein who is a doctor, trying to discover a way to make a living human using human body parts from the recently deceased. He succeeds, but his creation is looked upon as a monster by the local inhabitants, who wish to destroy it.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Spacemonster
programme.desc: When an atomic war on Mars destroys the planet's women, it's up to Martian Princess Marcuzan and her right-hand man Dr. Nadir to travel to earth and kidnap women for new breeding stock. Landing in Puerto Rico, they shoot down a NASA space capsule manned by an android. With his electronic brain damaged, the android terrorizes the island while the Martians raid beaches and pool parties.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Wolf Man
programme.desc: The resurrected Wolf Man, seeking a cure for his malady, enlists the aid of a mad scientist, who claims he will not only rid the Wolf Man of his nocturnal metamorphosis, but also revive the frozen body of Frankenstein's inhuman creation.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: House of Dracula
programme.desc: A scientist working on cures for rare afflictions, such as a bone softening agent made from molds to allow him to correct the spinal deformity of his nurse, finds the physical causes of lycanthropy in wolf-man Larry Talbot and of vampirism in Count Dracula, but himself becomes afflicted with homicidal madness while exchanging blood with Dracula.

programme.title: House of Frankenstein
programme.desc: After escaping from prison, the mad Dr. Gustav Niemann and his loyal hunchbacked assistant, Daniel, revive not only Count Dracula, but the Wolf Man and Frankenstein's Monster as well, all in order to exact revenge upon Niemann's many enemies.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: An American who sells cars in England receives a mysterious invitation from an old, eccentric millionaire to visit his house in which he lives with his twin brother.

programme.title: House of Wax
programme.desc: A sculptor opens a wax museum to showcase the likenesses of famous historical figures, but quickly runs into trouble when his business partner demands the exhibits become more extreme in order to increase profits.

programme.title: I Bury the Living
programme.desc: Through a series of macabre "coincidences," the newly-elected director of a cemetery (Richard Boone) begins to believe that he can cause the deaths of living owners of burial plots by merely changing the push-pin color from white (living) to black (dead) on a large wall map of the cemetery that notes those plots.

programme.title: Aerosmith Live Osaka, Japan 1999 Millennium Countdown 2000
Paul McCartney Back In The US
Lennon Legend - The Very Best Of John Lennon
Led Zeppelin Live Royal Albert Hall
The Who - Live At The Isle Of Wight Festival (1970)
ACDC - Live at Caste Donington, England (August 17, 1991)
The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Sophia goes into action to break her friend, Lillian out of ""the worst nursing home in the city."" However, Lillian proves to be too much of a handful for Sophia to handle. Meanwhile, Blanche considers having her breasts enlarged.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: After their ex-roommate Dorothy moves away to Atlanta with her new husband, Blanche, Rose, and Sophia sell their house and decide to purchase a dilapidated hotel near the beach.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: Wine, Women and Aunt Esther (a.k.a.) Leaving the Nest
programme.desc: Fred and his buddies are down over the death of a close friend and Aunt Esther's over-reacting does nothing to help. Thus, Fred decides to throw a cheer-up party and intends to have it in the Sanford home, complete with booze, music and friends of Fast Fanny, a topless waitress.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie and Edith are shocked when they discover that Stephanie has been stealing some of her favorite teacher's belongings, and the Bunkers's also. But Stephanie admits that she stole these things so that when she returns to her dad's, she'll have something to remember them by.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: When Barney's wife finally desserts him for good, Archie decides to fix him up with Edith's friend, who happens to be a very wealthy widow. When they seem to click and enter into a steady relationship, Edith has concerns that Barney is only after the woman's money.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: When Archie and his brother decide to partner up together in a business venture, his brother comes to the Bunker home to discuss the plans. Archie is both surprised and outraged when his brother arrives with his new wife, a child bride of 18.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Rolynn & Kelly vs. Shelly & Leslie vs. Melissa & Joe
programme.desc:
Sweep of Champions, Day 2
Rolynn & Kelly is the same year in July 13, 1992.
Shelly & Leslie is the same year in June 7, 1991.
Melissa & Joe is the same year in July 7, 1992.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Catherine & Taylor vs. Howard & Laura vs. Erika & Suzanne
programme.desc:
Sweep of Champions, Day 3
Catherine & Taylor is the same year in August 18, 1992.
Howard & Laura is the same year in July 2, 1991.
Erika & Suzanne is the same year in August 11, 1992.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Moe, a boxing manager, discovers that when Larry plays "Pop Goes the Weasel" on his violin, Curly goes berserk and punches any available target. Moe offers to manage Curly as the ring's newest boxing sensation and hires Larry to play his violin ringside. At the championship fight, Larry's violin is broken and he frantically searches the streets for an alternate source of music.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are tricked into becoming stowaways by their neighbor "Borscht", a spy for an enemy country. Stranded on a freighter on the high seas, they discover that their friend has concealed some stolen microfilm in watermelons they brought aboard for him. After a wild chase, they subdue Borscht and recover the microfilm.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: When the stooges are left to do their income taxes together one day, they decide to get lots of money by defrauding the government with tax schemes. Once they are living richly they throw a party in which the main guest ends up being a government official who tells them the jig is up.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges graduate from dental school and go out west to open a practice. Everything goes well until Shemp "cures" an outlaw's toothache from the instructions in a carpentry book, and the boys must leave on the run.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are delivering some Arabian antiques, which include a magic lamp complete with genie. Three Arabian bad guys are after the magic lamp, but the stooges defeat them once they get the "genius", (as Shemp calls the genie) on their side.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges don't know it, but they are all engaged to the same girl, a gold-digger who plans to get an engagement ring from each of them and then abandon them. When all three show up at her house at the same time, a wild fight ensues, as each stooge accuses the others of making time with "his" girl. The gold-digger gets it in the end (literally) with tacks shot from a repeating rifle.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are government agent entrusted with protecting professor Snead, who has invented a super rocket fuel. Larry is mistaken for the professor by foreign agents who kidnap the trio and take them to the country of Anemia where they are ordered to produce the rocket fuel or be executed. The boys come up with a concoction they try to pass of as the real stuff, but are exposed when the real professor and his daughter are also kidnapped. The stooges help them escape, using their secret formula to fuel a jeep.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: Tonight on MeTV- with summertime only a couple months away, we thought it’d be a good idea to remind you that’s when insects again become an annoyance- but not to the extent of the man-eating menace that defrosts and creates a one-bug infestation- as an actor we know from a certain classic legal drama tries to win the case against- "The Deadly Mantis!"

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: A troubled writer moves into a haunted house after inheriting it from his aunt.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The new wife next door, Kathy Bowman, looks down on Roseanne and complains that D.J. is a bad influence on her son Todd.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne helps Crystal with her Lamaze classes; Dan's near-fatal accident at work makes D.J. follow him obsessively; Dan and Roseanne sit up all night doing their wills, and Jackie is offended that she doesn't get custody of the kids.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne tries to convince Darlene to wear something feminine to a big school dance -- but she'd rather attract attention for her softball skills.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The family barbecue sees Nana Mary's first appearance on the show. Family and friends (except Dan's father and Roseanne's mother) gather at the Conner household for food, drink and singing, and Roseanne's fellow waitress Bonnie, gets a chance to show what a great singer she really is. Beverley still manages to upset Roseanne and Jackie from the other end of the phone, but Nana Mary turns the tables on her.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan and Roseanne commit to opening a motorcycle shop with a free-spirited old friend who gets cold feet at the last minute.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne is stunned when Becky asks for advice about birth control.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan open up shop at Lanford Custom Cycle, but selling a bike proves an exhausting challenge, as does keeping the household fine-tuned with Becky in charge.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Jackie awakens and is horrified to find that she has slept with Arnie; Dan invites Leon home for the weekly poker game; D.J. trades his comics for a dog.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: When Mr. Grace decides to open the store early, the staff set out to sabotage the new policy.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc: The "Toon In With Me" studio becomes overrun with mysterious green goo.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: An art critic, Dale Kingston, kills his uncle for his valuable collection of paintings. All the clues lead to the dead man's first wife. Can Columbo correct the Lieutenant's error before it is too late?

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: At a new job, Maude manages three men who find it hard to work for a woman.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude is spending much of her time with a best-selling author and Walter resents her new-found friendship.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: A week prior to a friend's annual party, the girls debate on what they should do to improve their appearance. This leads the girls to reminisce about previous attempts to improve their looks.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Sophia's old friends, Philomena and Dominic, come to Miami from Sicily, for a visit and have shocking news for Dorothy. Philomena and Sophia had met in the hospital when they were giving birth and now Philomena and Dominic claim that there was a mix-up and Dorothy was switched at birth. Meanwhile, Blanche and Rose take ""dirty dancing"" lessons.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As Rhaenyra looks to gain an advantage by unusual means, Daemon pressures a young liege lord to raise up his bannermen.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: The High Sparrow eyes another target. Jaime confronts a hero. Arya makes a plan. The North is reminded.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: While Jaime weighs his options, Cersei answers a request. Tyrion's plans bear fruit. Arya faces a new test.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Cameron postpones her vacation with Chase in order to ask House to accept the case of an environmental radical who collapsed at a protest with unexplainable symptoms. Although suspicious of her motives, House agrees. Since she pushed him to take the case so emphatically, House forces Cameron to take the lead and run many of the tests on the patient. Meanwhile, House is unsure of Wilson’s new healthy diet.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House's sleep deprivation is beginning to take its toll. He's constantly hallucinating Amber and even needs "her" to come up with ideas to find out what's wrong with a deaf wrestler who during a match started "hearing" loud explosions. Meanwhile, House prepares Chase's bachelor party, complete with strippers and lots of alcohol.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Marge leaves the house to recuperate from a skating injury, everything goes to hell - until Lisa convinces Homer and Bart they have leprosy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer's rush to the hospital to reattach his thumb, Lisa's rush to school to win the science fair, and Bart's run-in with an illegal fireworks scheme are interconnected in a parody of Go and Run Lola Run.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Twisted World of Marge Simpson
programme.desc: Marge goes to work for Frank Ormond,making and selling pretzels and,when he dies,sets up in business for herself. Initially sales are poor until Homer brings in local Mafia boss Fat Tony as her business partner. Sales boom as a result but,unfortunately,any profits vanish due to Fat Tony's 'business agreement' with Homer.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer feuds with the Springfield garbage men and decides to run for Sanitation Commissioner. After winning the election with a sleazy campaign, Homer can’t keep the extravagant promises he made to the town.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Having foiled an attempt by ex-convict Snake to rob the family one night Marge joins the local police force and becomes extremely zealous,dishing out punishments to the family and breaking up Homer's illegal gambling school. When Homer's friend Herman gets him involved in a scam to sell stolen jeans Marge tries to arrest him but gets no help from Wiggum and eventually resigns,complaining of police corruption.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Feeling that her own kids take her for granted,Marge takes pity on Nelson,whose dad has walked out on him and his loose-living mother,and lets him stay at her house. Bart is horrified as it means he gets bullied at home as well as at school. There is only one thing for it. Bart goes out and finds Nelson's father,bringing happy outcomes all round.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A hurricane destroys the Flanders' home and they have to move into the church basement. The people of Springfield gather to rebuild their house, but when Ned sees the poor workmanship, he has a complete breakdown.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Bart can't pay the credit card bill for his expensive new purebred dog, the repo men unwittingly take Santa's Little Helper.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Springfield is officially declared the World's Fattest Town after an attempt to break a world record lands everyone on top of a truck scale. Out of embarrassment and disgust, Marge goes on a crusade against the local sugar corporation. However, when sugar is banned, Homer, Bart, Mr. Burns and Apu start bootlegging sugar.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: To get out of cleaning a park as community service, Homer fakes his own death. When this results in the family's utilities being cut off, Marge puts pressure on him to reveal that he is alive. However, the spurious news of his "death" brings his long-lost mother - a hippie who is on the run from the law because of her activism against Mr. Burns - back to Springfield.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: With "Stone Cold" Steve Austin's spot in the main event at WrestleMania now secured, Mr. McMahon looks to make the show even bigger by revealing a special referee. The Rock challenges WWE Champion Mick Foley to a Ladder Match in one last effort to bring home the gold. Plus, X-Pac defends the European Championship and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny offers to help out an injured Twyla by waiting tables at the café, but soon gets in over his head. Alexis and Moira make a plan to have lunch, despite the fact that they're both anxious about spending time alone together. David and Stevie try to figure out the boundaries of their three-way relationship with Jake.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny decides to buy a new car, and he and Moira craft an increasingly elaborate ruse to avoid getting gouged by the dealership. David accompanies Stevie as she says goodbye to her great-aunt and confronts her own mortality in the process. Alexis and Ted negotiate the rules of their professional relationship when Ted receives a gift from a female client.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline find out that one of their customers at the diner is actually a successful photographer who has been keeping a secret.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Sophie’s wedding shower is interrupted by a hostage situation and Max and Caroline’s cupcake business gets unwanted attention when a tenant is arrested while wearing their company’s T-shirt.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A handsome businessman returns Max and Caroline’s missing cat, Nancy, who comes home with a big surprise.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to bring in extra money to help pay off their bank loan by taking second jobs as a pastry chef and a hostess at a chic restaurant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max pursues Nashit, a handsome new waiter at The High, despite Joe’s “no hook-up” rule.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Sophie takes Max and Caroline shopping for their bridesmaids’ dresses, the girls enlist the diner gang’s help to hide the ugliest ones, in the hopes of avoiding complete and utter humiliation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Look Of The Irish
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to break Nash into the modeling business in an effort to bring in extra money acting as his managers. Also, Sophie and Oleg attempt to keep celibate for 30 days before their wedding.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline’s attempt to break Nash into the modeling business comes to a screeching halt when his mother arrives from Ireland to retrieve her son.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline help launch the new branch of The High at JFK airport, and Sophie discovers that Oleg may have an ulterior motive for wanting to marry her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are tasked with retrieving Sophie’s wedding dress from customs and safely transporting it to the church in time for her big wedding.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline team up with the diner staff in a frantic attempt to keep their businesses open when the city targets Han's diner and their cupcake window for destruction. Also, Sophie and Oleg announce that they are trying to have a baby.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When the shower at Max and Caroline's apartment breaks, they get part-time jobs at a fancy gym's juice bar and enjoy free use of the locker room. However, their jobs and free showers are jeopardized when Caroline has a run-in with a group of rich moms who are jealous of the attention she's getting from a sexy trainer.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline's ex Andy returns and she is denial over how they broke up. And when he informs her that he's about to get married, she offers to make the wedding cake. And she is constantly wondering if she made a mistake breaking up with him. And she tries to find wrong with him but can't.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's cupcake shop is boycotted after they refuse to sell cupcakes to "I", an artist who wants to put cocktail weenies in them.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Han insists the diner gang attends a mandatory night out in an "Escape Room Club" when he feels they aren't working together as a team. However, once they're locked inside the "Alice in Wonderland"-themed room, they find Han has an ulterior motive.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Not Regular Down There
programme.desc: Max is both puzzled and curious when the new guy she is dating expresses hesitations about intimacy because he is "not regular down there." Also, Sophie and Oleg struggle to get pregnant.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: With things turning around for Richmond, it's time for everyone to work on their issues—like Ted's discomfort, Nate's confidence, and Roy's attention.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Ted and Dr. Sharon realize they might have to meet each other halfway. Tensions are high as the team prepares for the semifinal.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: As Thanksgiving approaches, Tony's feud with Ralph escalates, despite the intervention of Johnny Sack. At a frat-house mixer, Meadow and Jackie Jr. find they have more in common than mob-boss dads.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony gives Carmela a sapphire ring for her birthday...and Christopher gives Adriana her very own music club. Anthony Jr. gets busted for vandalizing the school swimming pool, but finds the punishment doesn't fit the crime. Tony test-drives a new Mercedes--and the Mercedes saleswoman, Gloria.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.sub-title: The Funeral to End All Funerals
programme.desc: As the Judge hears Michael and Shawn's arguments about the results of the experiment, Janet and the humans give each other the funerals they deserved.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: With time running out to find answers, Chidi must sort through his life -- and afterlives -- before he can provide the help everyone needs.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: The group scrambles to devise a new afterlife system that the Good and Bad Places can agree on before it's too late. Chidi has a newfound confidence.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As Aemond becomes more volatile, Larys plots an escape, and Alicent grows more concerned about Helaena's safety. Flush with new power, Rhaenyra looks to press her advantage.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Randy Savage becomes the "Macho King" at his coronation as "King of WWE." "Rowdy" Roddy Piper, The Widow Maker, Demolition and more compete in the ring. Hercules faces Mr. Perfect. The Brain Busters take on The Bushwhackers.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: After a family vote rules against buying a new couch during TV Night, Louise takes matters into her own hands. The Belchers take sides as they are forced to decide whether memories are enough to keep their family sofa. Will they take the Sofa Queen’s advice and learn to love a new couch?

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray finds out that the diamond on Debra's engagement ring is a fake, so he steals it from her to get it changed, only to find out after that she already had it exchanged it with her grandma's $15,000 diamond.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray and his family play a game of Scruples after their cable goes out, they find themselves arguing about honesty. To make matters worse, when Ray complains to the cable company about the disruption in service, he is informed that he has an illegal hookup.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug Heffernan takes $100 from the emergency fund jar to bet on boxing, and wins $5000 with Deacon. But they realize they can't tell anybody or Carrie will know he took the money, and Kelly will try to get the money in the divorce. They decide the best thing to do is blow all the money on the best day ever.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug has a bad tooth and decides to go to Deacon's dentist. But when Doug finds out that Carrie and the dentist know each other, he feels that the Dr. is hurting Doug on purpose out of jealousy. When Deacon wins a poker game over Arthur and Arthur doesn't have the money he promised him, Arthur tells him that he will give his son free piano lessons. The only problem is that Arthur doesn't know how to play the piano.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George goes out of his way to seek an apology for an insult; when Elaine is insulted that a fellow co-worker is scared of her germs, Puddy admits that he's a fellow germaphobe; Jerry dates a nudist and learns the difference between good naked and bad naked; Kramer installs a garbage disposal in his shower.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer returns to H&H bagels after a 12 year strike. Elaine gives "denim vest" a fake number on her free sub card. Jerry meets a woman named Gwen who is "two faced". (She doesn't look good in some light)George creates a fake charity called "the human fund" to give fake Christmas presents at Kruger. He invites Kruger over to celebrate "Festivus" when he can't cash the 20,000 dollar charity check to "the human fund".

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne is mad at Dan for forgetting Valentine's Day, so he winds up full-figure lingerie shopping; and Darlene's upset when a boy she likes asks out Becky instead.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne is torn between taking care of her flu-stricken husband and helping Jackie learn lines for her play.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael stages an elaborate plan to finally get Pam and Jim over to his and Jan's place for a dinner party. Jim gets trapped into it and agrees to go. Dwight overhears and wants his invitation, too. Michael says he only has six wine glasses and with Andy and Angela going too, there's not enough room for Dwight.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Pam is becoming more and more annoyed with Michael and his indecisiveness about ordering a desk chair. In Michael's defense, he's become distracted by his crush on the woman who is modeling the chair in the catalogue. He reminds Pam that since the infamous dinner party, he is now a single man.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Ryan returns to Scranton but not to a warm welcome. He is making everyone in the branch come in on a Saturday to re-enter data into the new website. Michael makes it clear that Ryan is the reason they have to come in on the weekend. The rest of the staff aren't happy knowing a computer will be getting credit for all of their sales.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Two Cars in Every Garage and Three Eyes on Every Fish
programme.desc: Bart catches a three-eyed fish in the river near Springfield Nuclear Power Plant. When the fish story is featured prominently in the local news, the plant is inspected and then fined millions of dollars for its many safety violations. Infuriated, Mr. Burns decides to run for governor so he can do away with the regulations that are costing him so much money.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer fires up the crowd at a Springfield Isotopes baseball game and is chosen to be the team's new mascot. He immediately becomes a popular attraction and the Isotopes start a winning streak. As a result, Homer is promoted to a team in Capital City, the Capital City Capitals.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Todd Flanders decide to enter a miniature golf tournament. Homer becomes confident that Bart will win and makes a bet with Ned that the father of the boy that does not win will have to mow their neighbors lawn in their wife's Sunday dress.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa makes a table centerpiece for the Thanksgiving dinner, which Bart accidentally destroys. After he is sent to his room by his parents, Bart angrily runs away from home and experiences a very different kind of Thanksgiving holiday.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons go to a Monster truck rally that features famous daredevil Lance Murdock. Bart immediately becomes enamored and decides that he wants to become a daredevil as well. His first stunt ends in injury, but to gain the respect of his friends, he promises a far more impressive feat to them: to jump the Springfield Gorge.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Against Al's objection, Peggy buys a home computer for the kids, to help them with their schoolwork, but it gets more use as a hat rack when no one wants to use it. Al begins to hear the computer talking to him and taunting him about his inferior life. At the same time, Al tries to train the not-so-bright Buck to fetch his bedroom slippers.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Seeking a tad more family togetherness (like TV's "Eight Family") and a family picture, Al declares "Bundy Day at the Beach", in which he takes the family to a local beach on Lake Michigan. Bud and Kelly compete with each other to find some dates for themselves, while an old flame, Marilyn Beamis, finds Al.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When all the women in the neighborhood gets peeped on except Peggy, she gets really jealous and eventually puts out a ladder, milk, and cookies for the him. Meanwhile Al convinces Bud to stop fooling Kelly and actually help her with her studies.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: In the infamous "Lost Episode", Al and Peg learn they have been videotaped being intimate at a sleazy motel. The same thing has also happened to Steve and Marcy. They decide to take the matter to court.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: On Labor Day, the gender roles are reversed when Al becomes the loafer, while he forces Peggy to prepare the backyard barbecue for the creation of his famous "Bundy Burgers." But one of the secrets of their flavor is the ashes of Marcy's dead aunt, which Kelly stole from the Rhoades' house.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peggy wins a two-week exercise course with local TV personality Jim Jupiter, "the healthiest man in Chicago," which soon turns into a test of wills between Peggy and the TV aerobics instructor. Jim succumbs to Peggy's lazy lifestyle of smoking and eating junk food. When Jim Jupiter literally drops dead afterward, during his TV show, Peggy surprisingly begins to suffer from a rare form of guilt.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Kelly and Bud need to raise money for concert tickets for themselves, so Bud sells Al as a camping guide, and Kelly cheats at a poker game with Peggy's friends. With Steve and Bud along for the ride, Al has to take a bunch of kids camping and they end up stranded in the woods and only Buck can save them.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Julio has gotten tired of the junk business and that's when Lamont comes up with an idea, Sanford & Son Salvage should merge with Julio's business. Julio goes for the idea but Fred is against it, but they merge anyway to form Sanford & Fuentes Salvage. However, everyone soon realizes the consequences attached to such a decision.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont's friend Herman has just gotten out of prison and doesn't have a place to stay. So Lamont invites him to stay at the Sanford residence. However, when Grady learns that he's an ex-con, he nails down everything in the house. Meanwhile, Lamont finds Herman a job at Esther's hardware store and doesn't tell her that he has served time.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont comes home one evening and discovers Fred preparing a gourmet dinner and learns it's to celebrate the arrival of an old friend of Fred's, Bowlegs, a musician, who's visiting. Bowlegs' act is to be put on at a nightclub but when his partner strains his back, Fred and Lamont must fill in to fulfill the nightclub's requirements.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont's politically involved girlfriend stresses involvement in the community's problems. This leads Lamont to phone into a radio talk show to express his views. Positive reaction leads to Lamont deciding to run for state assembly. But Fred soon finds out that this means that Lamont may move to Sacramento and leave the junk business.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: The cast appears on Wheel and Deal, hosted by Harry Monty.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris finds proof that makes him think he is adopted, so he sets off to find his natural parents, an Amish couple.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris goes to work for the police in a sting operation after he buys a counterfeit watch, the Chrono-Sync 2000.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky returns peoples dropped items to get enough money for an ice cream soda.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky and his pet ostrich, Lulu, get invited to perform on Broadway for $75/week.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky is in the foreign legion.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: At race day, Porky finds himself up against famous stars such as Laurel and Hardy, and Charlie Chaplin.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky has a dream about what it would be like to be married to Petunia.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A typical afternoon at the movies is lampooned in this looney trip to the cinema.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: New duck hunter Porky is constantly taunted by a very early version of Daffy, and all the other ducks.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky Pig and Dirty Digg, two building contractors, are in a war for the deal to build the town hall.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head find an ATM card, get some cash with it and get hunted by the law on TV.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Raccoons steal Beavis and Butt-Head's nachos, and the boys fight back.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head finally manage to get their hands on some (non-alcoholic) beer and get "fit-shaced".

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys go to a carnival sideshow to watch Tiffany The Rubber Band Lady perform. Somehow, they wind up in their own show, as the "Chicken-Head-Biting, Siamese twin Geeks."

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
A manners expert visits the class, and after attempting to teach Beavis & Butt-head manners, loses it and gets in a fight with Mr. Van Driessen. videos:
Green Day - "Longview"
Prong - "Snap Your Fingers, Snap Your Neck"
Styx - "Too Much Time On My Hands"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys accidentally score highly on an IQ test and wind up in the gifted class, where they destroy an actual gifted kid's brain.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis gets a nose bleed and tries just about everything to get it to stop.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After the TV goes out, the boys sleep through four disasters that ravage their town.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Two babes want the boys to change their flat tire, Butt-head learns what not to do with a tire-iron.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Our heroes are forced to go on Captain Dick Jackman's talk show and get frustrated when they don't score with some girls named Lolita & Tanqueray.
"Woke Up With A Monster" (Cheap Trick)
"If That's Your Boyfriend" (Me'Shell Ndege'Ocello)
"Bad To The Bone" (George Thorogood)

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis & Butt-head visit a phony fortune teller who cons Beavis into thinking he has the gift of second sight.
videos:
Metallica - "For Whom the Bell Tolls"
Nina Hagen - "Herman Was His Name"
Donald Fagen - "Snowbound"

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: The secret word is "begin." It's Spring - Cowboy Curtis stops by and he and Pee-wee plant a seed and watch it grow. The King of Cartoons brings his wife (The Queen of Cartoons) and their new baby (The Prince of Cartoons) over and introduces them to Pee-wee.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Aaaaugh! The secret word is "zyzzybalubah," and that can only mean one thing! The entire Playhouse, including Reba, is sucked into space, transported to a strange planet, and taken over by an odd, lonely alien! Yvona, an alien from the planet, is trying to escape the unfriendly Zyzzybalubah, but Pee-wee teaches him how to be polite and make friends. Reba teaches Pee-wee how to whistle, and she sings a song.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: As Max pet-sits Randy’s dog, Bruno, and Caroline sets up meetings with actresses who are interested in playing her in the potential biopic, Bruno escapes while a cannibalistic serial killer is loose in their neighborhood.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The girls’ Hollywood adventure continues as Caroline signs away the rights to her life story so it can be turned a movie. Also, Max is heartbroken when Randy breaks up with her via his therapist, Elliot, and Caroline rents a party bus in an attempt to cheer her up.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Show and Don’t Tell
programme.desc: When the girls go to prison to see Caroline’s father, Martin, perform in a musical, they tell him their plan to use Caroline’s movie money to convert their cupcake window into a dessert bar.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: The Hart Foundation take on The Powers of Pain in the featured match. Akeem, “Ravishing” Rick Rude and “Brutus” The Barber Beefcake are in action. The Genius and Mr. Perfect pontificate on “The Brother Love Show”.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons play Dodge or Dare (Dodj or Daar).

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Mom lets the Wattersons have it in a supermarket.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Gumball and Darwin receive a scary email, everyone is a suspect... including themselves!

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin has promised to play video games with Gumball, but there is a Banana Joe-shaped problem...

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Nicole is away "anything goes" and Richard's lack of rules soon attracts unwelcome house guests.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin try to understand Juke's urgent beatbox message.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: A new girl, Sarah, causes trouble for Gumball and Darwin when she tries to get the guys from her old school to fight them. Gumball and Darwin aren't having it.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball decides to take down the Internet after he accidentally creates an embarrasing viral web video.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Watterson kids come up with a fool-proof plan to save Nicole, but is it Gumball-proof?

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The life of the "inanimate" objects of Elmore are shown in a series of short skits.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: The Day the Night Stopped Beginning to Shine and Became Dark Even Though It Was the Day - Chapter Two: The Story in Your Eyes
programme.desc: Without Cyborg, Beast Boy feels lost and sets out to find himself, but ends up meeting a music legend.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: The Day the Night Stopped Beginning to Shine and Became Dark Even Though It Was the Day - Chapter Three: Playing Hard to Get
programme.desc: The Titans return to the alternate reality to save Cyborg but will they be able to defeat the dragon?

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: The Day the Night Stopped Beginning to Shine and Became Dark Even Though It Was the Day - Chapter Four: The Night Begins to Shine
programme.desc: In a final epic battle, the Titans and their musician friends square off against the dragon for control of the Night Begins to Shine.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin introduces the other Titans to a money-making application but they soon learn the dangers of a shareconomy.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin uses Labor Day as an opportunity to teach the other Titans about the importance of labor.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Control Freak attempts to prove his point about classic superhero cartoons by sending the Titans to a former version of "Teen Titans Go!"

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans build an AI robot so it will create a pizza that's never been made before.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman and Commissioner Gordon have a slumber party and watch their favorite TV shows.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman and Commissioner Gordon have a slumber party and watch their favorite TV shows.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Justice League's Next Top Talent Idol Star (1)
programme.desc: The Justice League holds a talent competition to find a superhero worthy of joining their team and Robin tries everything to win.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren is suffering from horrible allergies. Little does he know that he may be allergic to his best pal Stimpy.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy find an abandoned circus clown at their doorstep. While Ren DOES NOT want to keep him, Stimpy pleads and begs until Ren agrees.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy get jobs as bellhops and must make sure that no one finds out who is living in the top two floors of the hotel, but their curiosity gets the best of them.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy starts his journey into teenagedom, complete with all of its trials & tribulations.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren thinks TV has ruined Stimpy's mind, but when Stimpy wins a televised Gritty Kitty Litter contest and becomes a Hollywood star on the Muddy Muddskipper show, he leaves Ren behind.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
While searching for a missing kid, Beavis & Butt-head stumble upon a golf game with Mr. Anderson, then follow him around the golf course to steal his golf balls and sell them to other golfers.
videos:
Primal Scream - "Rocks"
Sonic Youth - "Bull In The Heather"
KMFDM - "A Drug Against War"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis & Butt-head go to the Medical Center. Butt-head gets nitrous at the dentist, Beavis gets an eye exam and a sucker.
videos:
DJ Jazzy Jeff & The Fresh Prince - "I Wanna Rock"
Kate Bush - "Love & Anger"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Our heroes are forced to go on Captain Dick Jackman's talk show and get frustrated when they don't score with some girls named Lolita & Tanqueray.
"Woke Up With A Monster" (Cheap Trick)
"If That's Your Boyfriend" (Me'Shell Ndege'Ocello)
"Bad To The Bone" (George Thorogood)

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis and Butt-head man a crisis line for Mr. Van Driessen and try to "get some" from a caller by being polite.
videos:
Danzig - "Mother '93"
James - "Say Something"
Mutha's Day Out - "Locked"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis and Butt-head vs. The Vending Machine
programme.desc:
Beavis and Butt-head try various means to get their bag of snacks unstuck and out of the vending machine before they starve. videos:
Sweaty Nipples - "Demon Juice"
Elton John & RuPaul - "Don't Go Breakin' My Heart"
Seaweed - "Kid Candy"
Motley Crue - "Houligan's Holiday"

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Skyscrapers (Deanna & Joey) vs. Crushing Duo (Stacy & David)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Real People will not be seen at this time because we couldn't find any. In its place, we present the following unfair program.
The cast complains about everything that is unfair with the world in 1982. To add insult to injury, the Unfairy Godmother stops by.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
My Three Sons of Frankenstein will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine tells us about all of her greatest fears. In the sketches, the cast talks about the pros and cons of getting older.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Kermit the Frog Tries to Cross the Highway will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast looks at all of the pets people have, some normal, some very strange.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mister Rogers' Neighborhood will not be seen because the neighborhood has been declared unsafe.
Ross forces the cast to follow new rules that the Union has made for child actors. Lisa is forced to eat a mud pie and loses her voice.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Laverne and Shirley Go to Finishing School will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The show's producers have written a book of manners, which Christine tries to explain.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
General Hospital will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
""Doctor"" Lisa and ""nurse"" Luke help Christine, who has broken her leg just before show time.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Life on Earth will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine learns to tell fortunes using a crystal ball. The cast also looks at the world of computers, and how they can help with the routines of daily life.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
You Can't Do That on Television will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
In a drastic effort to improve ratings, Ross has Kevin burned at the stake. In the sketches, the cast looks at how advertisers try to get our attention.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The following program contains scenes that may not be suitable for mature audiences. Juvenile discretion is advised.
The adult dressing room catches fire, and the kids have to put it out. Then the kids have to sacrifice their clothes so that the adults can resume shooting Richard III. The sketches are about how different kids and adults are treated.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: White Collar Careers vs. Blue Collar Careers
programme.desc:
It's Career Day on MXC as white collar workers and their proteges compete against the blue collar workers and their aide de camps.
Games include: Plank Spankers; Slipped Disks of Doom; Mud Butlers; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Comic Book Industry vs. Personal Hygiene
programme.desc:
MXC presents the battle between the people who prevent stinkers against the drawers with pen-and-inkers when the hygiene industry challenges the comic book industry. Also, Vic turns Kenny's unsuccessful Oral-Facializer into the even more unsuccessful Man-Ipulator, and we see the adventures of Megaphone Man when he goes into the lair of the evil doctor Medula Oblongata.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Highballers; Gang Plankers; Runaway Stump

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
In honor of Spike TV's relationship with the the Ultimate Fighting Championship, this special edition of MXC pits the ultimate fighters against the Wisteria Lane gang and their compadres of Desperate Housewives in the battle of the octagoners and the Eva Longorias.
Games include: Take a Leek; Mudd Butlers; Sinkers and Floaters; Dash to Death; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Real Mafia vs. Video Game Industry
programme.desc:
MXC presents the goombahs and the game boys (and girls) in this battle.
Games include: Backyard Bocci Ball Bloodbath; Dope on a Rope; Nerve Racketeering; Slabs of Death; Sorry-Wrong Number; Clear Sphere of Fear

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Hollywood's Jilted Wives vs. Cheating Husbands
programme.desc:
The Brads attempt to get the upper hand on the Jennifers in an all-star edition of MXC to kickoff 2006.
Games include: Rotating Surfboard of Death; Pole Riders; Hand Job; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Big Bird takes on big bets when MXC brings together two of the most polar opposites in America: The kiddie playground of Sesame Street and the adult playground known as Las Vegas.
Games include: Cruelette; Wall Buggers; Big Bird Droppings; Sinkers and Floaters

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Howard Stern's radio regulars go toe to toe against Hollywood rehab addicts and alkies...it's the classic battle of the wack pack vs. the crack pack. Games - Rotating Surfboards of Death, The Crate Escape, Sperm Whalers, and Sinkers and Floaters.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's 'Roid Rage' as the pumped up blood-doping Pro Athletes take on the Fashion industry's Super Models...it's the classic battle of Anabolic Steroids versus Anorexic mind voids! Games - Eat Shitake, Spit Ball, Irritable Bowl Syndrome, and Log Drop.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A labyrinthine discount-bulk-warehouse superstore serves as the destination for several characters. Jane goes there to buy Gummi Bears (for art supply purposes) after Tom ate hers. Daria accompanies her to buy new shoelaces, because Quinn stole the ones from her boot to repair a bag. Trent and Jesse go there to buy a headlamp and scented candles, respectively, and Tom accompanies them so he can find Jane and make up with her after their argument. (Walking with the two musicians, their half-brained purchase ideas drive him nearly up the wall.) The Fashion Club goes to the warehouse store to buy sunscreen and various other beauty needs, and, of course, their incredibly high standards prevent them from making any actual purchases. The free-sample cheese logs at the store become quite popular-an obsession, in fact, for hungry Mr. DeMartino, whose food budget is incredibly tight, and for Mrs. Johansen (a 300-pound hypoglycemic woman who is a recurring character). Kevin's dad, Doug, is holdi

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Is Lawndale a pretty little suburb, or an unholy nexus of evil haunted by the tortured denizens of the Beyond?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn discovers her spiritual side and we all have to live through it.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Jake suffers a slight kitchen mishap of the inferno variety, the Morgendorffers must move to a hotel.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria helps Jane dye her hair with less than stellar results.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale High is low on funds and the students and Faculty need to start cutting corners.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn reflects on anniversaries and her many admirers.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The fashion club sets new membership guidelines.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Wufgang Bah, the world's worst composer, has used a ray to destroy all the music on the planet so people will have to listen to his. Dangermouse and Penfold are on the case, using a cassette deck with the last remaining tape of background music, without which they are powerless.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold's hearing problems make him immune to a device that brainwashes anyone who hears Greenback's voice.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A spaceship has crash landed in Birmingham city center. DM and Penfold meet an alien who is looking for a small furry creature called a Ticklehipos, that just loves to climb up trouser legs and multiply.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Spy Who Stayed in With a Cold
programme.desc: Agent 57 teams up with Danger Mouse to defeat Baron Greenback when Penfold stays home with a cold; the only problem is 57 gets it too and every time he sneezes he changes disguise.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A new experimental enzyme being tested at the Wonder White Sub company laboratory bonds with an aggressive molecule and escapes down the drain. It starts attacking people, pinching underwear and tries to turn everything white.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold end up in a time-traveling clock when they investigate an attempted robbery of the crown jewels.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Electrocuted Hot Dog, Household Levers, Fly's Foot, Chocolate Journey, Watch, Mass vs Weight and more
programme.desc: Electrocuted Hot Dog, Household Levers, Fly's Feet, Magic Number, Oil Lamp, Chocolate Journey, Quartz Watch, Mass vs. Weight. Leila describes the various ways to cook a hot dog: boiling. fry, and baking in an oven. Mr. Wizard says they are going to electrocute one.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discusses the amoeba and uni-cellular animals called protozoa, reveals the secrets in the delicate diet of baby birds and shows the secrets behind hair-raising stunts.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman talks about horses and reveals the most disgusting animal in the world before describing just how does a refrigerator stays cold.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman shows the identifying structure of human fingerprints, and explains why humans fart, what this gaseous emission is made of and why it smells...

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Wacky scientist Beakman and his assistant Josie, and Lester, an actor dressed as a mangy rat, answers an inquiry about where puddles go after it has rained. Although Lester suggests they are licked up by stray dogs, Beakman proceeds to explain the mysteries of rain and evaporation using a variety of household props. In the "Beak-Mania" portion of the show, Beakman attempts to answer, in rapid succession, short viewer inquiries on topics such as living prehistoric animals (the platypus), the most commonly used word in the English language and the heaviest animal in the history of the world (blue whale). Then, after a trick in which he balances a pair of forks atop a toothpick, Beakman forces Josie to reveal the question for the show's final segment, "What is snot?" Turning to volcanoes, Beakman explains the processes in the Earth's interior that create, and then discharge, molten magma. With the aid of film footage of volcanic eruptions, Beakman employs a foam rubber model of the Earth and a rubber glove to show how magma moves from deep inside the Earth and appears on the surface as lava. Finally, asked by Josie about why some volcanoes explode, Beakman uses a cherry pie to demonstrate the power, and mess, of a volcanic eruption.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains the laws of gravity; Galileo shows the effects of gravity;. Sir Isaac Newton explains inertia; how a seat belt helps save lives.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: How do noses work? Objects give off tiny amounts of tiny molecules into the air. When just a few of these molecules get up your nose, they dissolve in the mucus up there. Some molecules come into contact with special receptors on what’s called your “olfactory membranes.” Each nostril has a membrane, and each membrane is only about the size of a postage stamp. The membranes hold millions of receptor cells, each of which are ready to send messages to the brain about the molecules that go up your nose.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Caves come in all different shapes and sizes depending on how they were formed. Spelunkers (people who explore caves) have plenty of descriptive names for caves and the natural rocky features that form within them – soda straws, fried eggs, popcorn and draperies are just a few. Caves can be as simple as one straight passage with a few stalactites overhead, or as complex as a labyrinth, with many stalagmite-carpeted rooms.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons are surprised to discover that their past actions have consequences.
